meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve no testing knowledge (Advanced Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no testing knowledge. A Advanced-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Master SaaS Pricing: Overcome Testing Gaps (Advanced Guide)
content.seo_description: Bridge the gap between no testing knowledge and professional SaaS pricing. Use Antigravity and Claude Code to build robust, scalable pricing logic for your app.
content.answer_block: You overcome the lack of testing knowledge by adopting 'Test-Driven Pricing Architecture.' By using Claude Code to generate unit tests alongside your pricing logic, you ensure that every subscription tier, discount, and edge case is mathematically verified, transforming untested code into production-grade financial infrastructure for your SaaS.
content.intro:
You’ve built the core features, the UI is slick, and the database is humming. But then you hit the wall: the financial engine. Without rigorous testing, your pricing logic is a ticking time bomb of revenue leaks and customer churn. Most students skip this because testing feels like a mountain, but in the real world, your pricing logic is the one place where 'close enough' equals bankruptcy.

Today, we stop guessing. We are going to treat your pricing model like a high-stakes financial transaction system. By leveraging AI-assisted test generation and structured architectural patterns, you will move from 'it works on my machine' to 'it is audited and bulletproof.' This is how you build the confidence to launch.

steps.title: Define the Pricing Schema
steps.description: Establish a rigid data structure for your tiers to prevent type-mismatch errors during checkout.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a TypeScript interface for a SaaS subscription model including base price, trial period, usage-based limits, and discount flags.

steps.title: Architect the Pricing Engine
steps.description: Decouple your pricing logic from your UI controllers to make it independently testable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a pure function 'calculateTotal' that accepts a plan object and usage metrics, returning the final invoice amount with tax handling.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --files 'src/logic/billing.ts' --command 'Refactor pricing logic into pure functions'

steps.title: Generate Property-Based Tests
steps.description: Use property-based testing to check hundreds of price combinations automatically.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a suite of unit tests using Vitest that validates the calculateTotal function against extreme edge cases like zero-usage, negative discounts, and overflow values.

steps.title: Simulate Revenue Scenarios
steps.description: Run simulations to ensure your logic handles currency conversion and discount stacking correctly.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a simulation script that mocks 500 different user subscription scenarios and verifies the output of our billing engine matches expected revenue.

steps.title: Integrate Stripe Webhook Verification
steps.description: Ensure your pricing logic is protected against malformed incoming data from your payment processor.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a validation middleware for Stripe webhooks that ensures the incoming event matches our internal pricing schema before processing.

pro_tips: Treat your pricing logic as a 'pure function' to eliminate side effects that cause hidden billing bugs.
Always use integer-based math (cents) instead of floats to avoid floating-point precision errors in financial calculations.
Implement 'Shadow Testing': run your new pricing engine in parallel with the old one and log discrepancies before the official swap.
Use Claude Code to document your 'why' in the code comments, not just the 'what', to ensure future maintainability.
Never trust the frontend; always re-verify the pricing calculation on the server-side immediately before creating a payment intent.
content.next_steps: Deploy your validated pricing engine to a staging environment and run a smoke test against a mock Stripe API key.
content.conversion_block: You now possess the architectural rigor to build financial systems that hold up under pressure. Use these patterns to turn your prototype into a professional-grade SaaS product. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
